response.justification: Candidate has extensive software development experience (20+ years) with strong relevant signals in developer tooling (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts library, MCP code generators, CI/CD infrastructure) and clear mentoring activity through workshops and courses. However, the resume shows no explicit people-management role, no formal direct reports, and only limited mobile platform exposure. Title progression stays at IC/Staff Engineer level, which is a meaningful gap for a Level II Manager role requiring 2+ years of people management.
strengths: Deep developer-tools background: built test runners, assertion libraries, CI infrastructure, and DevEx tooling
Strong mentoring/teaching footprint via multi-module Selenium course, Refactoring Dojos, and full-day workshops
Broad polyglot full-stack experience spanning web, mobile, embedded, and AI-agent infrastructure
concerns: No explicit people-management experience or direct-report leadership called out anywhere on the resume
Limited mobile-platform depth — only brief iOS/Apple TV mention from an early-career role
Overlapping employment dates and improbable early timeline (freelance from 1992) raise resume-accuracy questions
